game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Players drag and drop various geometric blocks onto a 9x9 grid, aiming to fill entire rows or columns to clear them and earn points, with the game ending when no more blocks can be placed from the current set of three available. Blocks cannot be rotated, and strategic placement is key to maximizing space and achieving high scores, with combo bonuses awarded for clearing multiple lines simultaneously or consecutively.
game_info.description: Block Blast! is a puzzle game where you place geometric blocks on a 9x9 grid. Your goal is to fill entire rows or columns to clear them and earn points. You have three blocks available at a time, and must strategically place them to maximize your score. The game ends when you can't place any of the available blocks on the grid. Earn combo bonuses by clearing multiple lines simultaneously or consecutively!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left/Right: Move the selected block horizontally
Arrow Up/Down: Move the selected block vertically
Z: Cycle through available blocks
Space: Place the selected block on the grid
Enter: Start the game from the start screen
Esc: Pause/unpause the game
R: Restart the game when game is over
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests that the game loads without errors and responds to player inputs using a random sequence of actions that changes periodically.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Randomly selects blocks and places them on the grid, switching between movement, selection, and placement actions to simulate normal gameplay.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game should run without errors, blocks should be placed on the grid, and the score should increase when rows or columns are completed.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>
<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests that a player can win the game by achieving a high score through strategic block placement.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Prioritizes placing blocks to complete rows and columns, focusing on creating opportunities for multiple line clears simultaneously.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The player should achieve a high score through efficient line clearing, demonstrating that skilled play leads to success.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>
<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests the game's ability to detect and handle filled lines correctly, including combo bonuses.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Places blocks specifically to create situations where multiple lines can be cleared simultaneously or consecutively.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Lines should clear properly when filled, points should be awarded correctly, and combo bonuses should be applied when multiple lines are cleared.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>
<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests the game's end condition by deliberately filling the grid in a way that no more blocks can be placed.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Places blocks to create a pattern that will eventually lead to no valid placements for any of the three available blocks.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game should correctly transition to the GAME_OVER_LOSE state when no more blocks can be placed from the current set.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>
<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests the block selection mechanism by cycling through available blocks and placing them strategically.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Frequently uses the block selection key (Z) to cycle through available blocks, finding the most optimal one for each placement.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game should correctly cycle through the three available blocks and allow placement of the selected block.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
